title: Commercial International Bank (CIB) (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Commercial International Bank (CIB) in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Commercial International Bank (CIB)?
faq.answer: Commercial International Bank (CIB) is Egypt's leading and largest private-sector bank, offering comprehensive financial products and services across multiple banking segments. The bank serves over 500 of Egypt's largest corporations, along with enterprises of all sizes, institutions, high-net-worth individuals, and retail customers. CIB has established itself as the most profitable private sector bank operating in Egypt, with a network exceeding 180 branches across the country.

faq.question: When was Commercial International Bank (CIB) founded?
faq.answer: CIB was founded in 1975 as a joint venture between the National Bank of Egypt (51%) and Chase Manhattan Bank (49%) under the original name "Chase National Bank of Egypt." In 1987, following Chase Bank's decision to divest its equity stake, the National Bank of Egypt increased its shareholding to 99.9%, and the bank was renamed Commercial International Bank. The bank's ownership structure evolved significantly through a privatization strategy that began in 1993 with a public share offering. In 2006, a consortium led by Ripplewood Holdings acquired the National Bank of Egypt's remaining stake. In 2009, Actis became the single largest shareholder, and in 2014, Fairfax Financial Holdings acquired a significant stake. In April 2022, ADQ, one of Abu Dhabi's sovereign funds, acquired an 18.595% stake worth $911.457 million.

faq.question: Where is Commercial International Bank (CIB) headquartered?
faq.answer: CIB is headquartered at the Nile Tower Building, 21/23 Charles De Gaulle, Giza, Cairo, Egypt. The headquarters serves as the central hub for the bank's operations across the country.

faq.question: Where does Commercial International Bank (CIB) operate?
faq.answer: CIB operates throughout Egypt with a well-established network of over 180 branches distributed across the country. The bank has a strong presence in major Egyptian cities and maintains extensive coverage to serve both urban and regional markets. CIB's branch network supports its diverse customer base, from major corporations in Cairo to retail customers in communities across Egypt.

faq.question: What services does Commercial International Bank (CIB) offer?
faq.answer: CIB offers a comprehensive range of banking services across multiple segments: Corporate Banking: Services for more than 500 of Egypt's largest corporations, including trade finance, cash management, and supply chain finance solutions. Commercial Banking: Products and services tailored for medium and large enterprises, with advanced digital corporate banking platforms. Retail Banking: Personal banking services including savings accounts, loans, mortgages, and payment solutions for individual customers. Wealth Management: Premium financial services for high-net-worth individuals. SME Banking: Dedicated products and support for small and medium-sized enterprises, with partnerships to facilitate business growth. Digital Banking: Advanced online banking platform with mobile banking applications, corporate channels with single-account access across devices, and secure authentication systems. The bank processes over 50% of corporate banking transactions through its online portal, reducing transaction processing time from 2 days to 2 hours and cutting per-transaction costs by 40%.

faq.question: What makes Commercial International Bank (CIB) unique?
faq.answer: CIB distinguishes itself through several key features: Digital Innovation Leadership: CIB was the first bank in Egypt to launch a supply chain finance platform, bringing buyers and suppliers together on a single digital platform. The bank has achieved 70% adoption of online banking services among corporate clients. Market Leadership: As Egypt's most profitable private-sector bank, CIB maintains the strongest brand equity in the Egyptian banking sector and is the bank of choice for over 500 of Egypt's largest corporations. Sustainability Commitment: CIB is an Equator Principles signatory and a member of the Net-Zero Banking Alliance, committing to reduce financed emissions to net zero by 2050. The bank is also part of the UNEP Finance Initiative and follows the Principles for Responsible Banking. Operational Excellence: The bank employs over 6,900 staff members and has successfully integrated advanced technology platforms from Finastra to deliver next-generation digital services. Strategic Acquisition: In late 2015, CIB acquired Citibank Egypt through the Egyptian Stock Exchange, incorporating all of its branches into CIB's operations.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Commercial International Bank (CIB)?
faq.answer: CIB is a Universal Bank operating across all major banking segments in Egypt. As a universal bank, CIB provides integrated financial services spanning corporate banking, commercial banking, retail banking, wealth management, and investment services. The bank operates under the supervision of the Central Bank of Egypt and is listed on the Egyptian Exchange (EGX), where it is included in the EGX 30 index of the country's leading companies. This classification reflects CIB's comprehensive service offering and its ability to serve the complete spectrum of banking customers, from individual retail clients to Egypt's largest corporations.
